Title: Viewing Profile Information
Preconditions: User is registered and logged into the application. Internet connection is active.
Test Steps:

2. Navigate to the profile page.
Expected Result: All information related to the user's account is displayed, including name, email, profile picture, preferences, activity history, and other settings.

Title: Updating Profile Information
Preconditions: User is registered and logged into the system. System is functioning and accessible.
Test Steps:

2. Modify the desired fields in the pre-filled profile update form.
3. Confirm the changes.
Expected Result: The user's information is updated and saved in the system.

Title: User Login Process
Preconditions: Application is installed and the user has registered.

2. Enter the credentials used during account creation.
3. Press the "Login" button.
Expected Result: The user is authenticated and redirected to the main screen of the application where they can access its features and services.

Title: Route Selection and Weather Information
Preconditions: User is registered and authenticated in the application. Application has access to the user's current location. System is connected to mapping and weather services.
Test Steps:

2. Select the starting and ending points of the route.
3. The system calculates and displays the route on the map.
4. The system requests weather conditions along the route.
5. Weather information is displayed at various points along the route.
Expected Result: The route and corresponding weather conditions are shown on the map.

Title: Viewing Detailed Route Information
Preconditions: User has completed a hike and saved the route in the app.

2. Choose the route whose details you want to view from the list of saved routes.
Expected Result: The user has access to all detailed information related to the selected route, including the map, estimated time, distance traveled, altitude variations, and other relevant statistics.

Title: Accessing Meteorological Information
Preconditions: User is registered and logged into the application.
Test Steps: 1. Access the application.
Expected Result:
The user can obtain accurate and timely information on weather phenomena that could affect their hiking route, enabling them to make informed decisions for safety and comfort.

Title: User requests weather information during a hike
Preconditions: User is logged into the application and has location services enabled.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a hike and requests weather information.
2. System acquires the current user's location.
3. System retrieves weather forecast for the specific area.
4. Weather information is processed and displayed in an easily understandable format.
5. System provides advice and recommendations based on the weather conditions.
Expected Result: User receives accurate weather information and relevant advice for their hike.

Title: User verifies a specific position and obtains local weather conditions
Preconditions: User is logged into the application and has location services enabled.
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on a specific position within the map displayed in the application.
2. System displays an indicator on the selected position.
3. System requests and shows local weather conditions for the selected position.
4. User verifies the position and weather information.
Expected Result: Local weather conditions for the selected position are correctly displayed.

Title: User views past workouts
Preconditions: User has completed at least one workout previously and is logged into the application.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the "Health" section of the platform.
2. System displays a chronological list of past workouts.
3. User selects a workout to view detailed statistics.
4. System provides information such as duration, steps taken, distance covered, and calories burned.
5. User filters workouts based on specific parameters (date, type of workout, etc.).
6. User deletes or shares specific workouts with other users if supported.
Expected Result: User successfully views and manages past workouts.

Title: User registers and monitors key statistics during a hike or workout
Preconditions: User is logged into the application.
Test Steps:
1. User starts the training functionality.
2. System starts a timer to track duration.
3. During the workout, system records number of steps, distance covered, and calories burned.
4. User interacts with the system to view real-time statistics.
5. At the end of the workout, user stops the registration.
6. System saves the statistics and updates the user profile.
Expected Result: Workout statistics are recorded and updated in the user profile. System is ready for a new training session.
